rules.when: strategic background, policy motivation, or two grouped context blocks
rules.guidance: Use two balanced evidence groups with an optional bottom synthesis line.

rules.when: methods, technical route details, or one/two explanatory figures
rules.guidance: Use compact method blocks and keep figure labels factual.

rules.when: three headline numbers carry the argument
rules.guidance: Show 3 metrics with short labels and one synthesis sentence.

rules.when: technical route has four phases or grouped pipeline steps
rules.guidance: Use four numbered phases with concise action verbs.

rules.when: technical route is a simple 3-step sequence
rules.guidance: Use horizontal 3-step progression from left to right.

rules.when: before/after, traditional/new, A/B, or鏂规瀵规瘮
rules.guidance: Use paired columns and end with a short synthesis.

rules.when: six related images or experiment panels are the main evidence
rules.guidance: Use 3x2 image rhythm; captions should be short.

rules.when: one strong result should dominate the slide
rules.guidance: Use one large finding plus optional context and source.

variants.best_for: default mixed text, figure, or custom academic composition
variants.layout: open CONTENT_AREA with PAGE_TITLE and KEY_MESSAGE supplied by the shell

variants.best_for: Situation background, policy context, strategic need, or challenge framing
variants.layout: two columns, each with a label, heading, 2-3 bullets, optional image, and a bottom slogan

variants.best_for: method details, model architecture, experiment setup, or one/two method figures
variants.layout: left and right method blocks, each pairing a concise heading, copy, and figure well

variants.best_for: research scale, dataset summary, benchmark results, or three KPI-like metrics
variants.layout: dominant figure or description area plus three metric cards

variants.best_for: four phase pipeline, grouped technical route, or implementation path
variants.layout: four numbered phases with optional icons and a top or bottom summary bar

variants.best_for: three step method, experiment process, or temporal flow
variants.layout: three horizontal steps with academic-blue number badges and teal connectors

variants.best_for: traditional versus proposed, baseline versus model, problem versus solution
variants.layout: two balanced columns with left/right labels, up to four points each, and a synthesis line

variants.best_for: 3x2 experiment panels, visual results, microscopy groups, or image comparisons
variants.layout: six equal image cells with captions and optional summary
variants.image_policy: preserve aspect ratio; crop only when the user asks

variants.best_for: core finding, key conclusion, one headline result, or transition into Answer
variants.layout: large declarative finding, teal accent bar, short context, optional source/figure
